generatedSentence: Lord Valerius, an egotistical artificer, held peremptory sway over his domain, though his internal workings hinted at a pathologic uremia of the soul. He once urged his undergraduate protégé, a graceful ballerina, to register his latest product: a synthetic unicorn, a mechanical fairy, designed to be the goal, a majestic striker for a new age. Instead, it became a maelstrom of problems, prompting him to deploy mouse-like commandos to an octagon-shaped venue, trying to lullaby the public with mere improvisation, picking at the threads of his failing ambition. His rival bros, an entrenched and barbaric faction, became envious, fueling a numbing rivalry that saw them awarded vast swaths of turquoise terrain along the waterfront. An impatient immigrant, fresh from a series of bad haircuts, found himself unworthy in this new landscape, constantly jaywalked by their high-speed jetski caravans on the freeway. He discovered a forgotten manuscript, detailing a critical revision to the unicorn's design, but it was lost somewhere on the highway, its only clue an eyelash trapped within its pages, sparking a nerdgasm of numerical calculations and multiplication strategies to combat them. The upcoming episode saw the immigrant, now an accidental tramp, empower a group of rebellious commandos. Their mission was to kickstart a greater neural network, the nexus of the artificer's true intentions, but it required crossing treacherous isles. They observed the enemy vicariously, their boomer keepers trading coins and muffins while listening to a lullaby, completely obtuse to the trading. The commandos, armed with nocked bows, used diffraction to camouflage their approach, their memory of the unicorn's original purpose making them stubbornly obedient. In the darkest laboratory, the artificer's daughter, once an overlooked assistant, revealed the solution. The unicorn's flyback circuit, responsible for its unique mango-like hue and a pathological overflow of energy, was intentionally sabotaged. With a single, decisive jab, she corrected the circuit, her efficiency a stark opposite to the previous infidelity. The unicorn, now free from troublesome code, responded to her wishes, its true form emerging as a vibrant, exciting spectacle, amidst falling raindrops. The former hawkers, now painters, set up their easels on the walkway, capturing the unicorn's beauty, a truly fantastical artichokes-inspired creature, as it began to wander, its powerful kickstarting a new era, securing the ultimate goal.
panelSentences: Lord Valerius, an egotistical artificer, held peremptory sway over his domain, though his internal workings hinted at a pathologic uremia of the soul. He once urged his undergraduate protégé, a graceful ballerina, to register his latest product: a synthetic unicorn, a mechanical fairy, designed to be the goal, a majestic striker for a new age. Instead, it became a maelstrom of problems, prompting him to deploy mouse-like commandos to an octagon-shaped venue, trying to lullaby the public with mere improvisation, picking at the threads of his failing ambition.

His rival bros, an entrenched and barbaric faction, became envious, fueling a numbing rivalry that saw them awarded vast swaths of turquoise terrain along the waterfront. An impatient immigrant, fresh from a series of bad haircuts, found himself unworthy in this new landscape, constantly jaywalked by their high-speed jetski caravans on the freeway. He discovered a forgotten manuscript, detailing a critical revision to the unicorn's design, but it was lost somewhere on the highway, its only clue an eyelash trapped within its pages, sparking a nerdgasm of numerical calculations and multiplication strategies to combat them.

The upcoming episode saw the immigrant, now an accidental tramp, empower a group of rebellious commandos. Their mission was to kickstart a greater neural network, the nexus of the artificer's true intentions, but it required crossing treacherous isles. They observed the enemy vicariously, their boomer keepers trading coins and muffins while listening to a lullaby, completely obtuse to the trading. The commandos, armed with nocked bows, used diffraction to camouflage their approach, their memory of the unicorn's original purpose making them stubbornly obedient.

In the darkest laboratory, the artificer's daughter, once an overlooked assistant, revealed the solution. The unicorn's flyback circuit, responsible for its unique mango-like hue and a pathological overflow of energy, was intentionally sabotaged. With a single, decisive jab, she corrected the circuit, her efficiency a stark opposite to the previous infidelity. The unicorn, now free from troublesome code, responded to her wishes, its true form emerging as a vibrant, exciting spectacle, amidst falling raindrops. The former hawkers, now painters, set up their easels on the walkway, capturing the unicorn's beauty, a truly fantastical artichokes-inspired creature, as it began to wander, its powerful kickstarting a new era, securing the ultimate goal.
rawPanelSentences: Lord Valerius, an egotistical artificer, held peremptory sway over his domain, though his internal workings hinted at a pathologic uremia of the soul. He once urged his undergraduate protégé, a graceful ballerina, to register his latest product: a synthetic unicorn, a mechanical fairy, designed to be the goal, a majestic striker for a new age. Instead, it became a maelstrom of problems, prompting him to deploy mouse-like commandos to an octagon-shaped venue, trying to lullaby the public with mere improvisation, picking at the threads of his failing ambition.

His rival bros, an entrenched and barbaric faction, became envious, fueling a numbing rivalry that saw them awarded vast swaths of turquoise terrain along the waterfront. An impatient immigrant, fresh from a series of bad haircuts, found himself unworthy in this new landscape, constantly jaywalked by their high-speed jetski caravans on the freeway. He discovered a forgotten manuscript, detailing a critical revision to the unicorn's design, but it was lost somewhere on the highway, its only clue an eyelash trapped within its pages, sparking a nerdgasm of numerical calculations and multiplication strategies to combat them.

The upcoming episode saw the immigrant, now an accidental tramp, empower a group of rebellious commandos. Their mission was to kickstart a greater neural network, the nexus of the artificer's true intentions, but it required crossing treacherous isles. They observed the enemy vicariously, their boomer keepers trading coins and muffins while listening to a lullaby, completely obtuse to the trading. The commandos, armed with nocked bows, used diffraction to camouflage their approach, their memory of the unicorn's original purpose making them stubbornly obedient.

In the darkest laboratory, the artificer's daughter, once an overlooked assistant, revealed the solution. The unicorn's flyback circuit, responsible for its unique mango-like hue and a pathological overflow of energy, was intentionally sabotaged. With a single, decisive jab, she corrected the circuit, her efficiency a stark opposite to the previous infidelity. The unicorn, now free from troublesome code, responded to her wishes, its true form emerging as a vibrant, exciting spectacle, amidst falling raindrops. The former hawkers, now painters, set up their easels on the walkway, capturing the unicorn's beauty, a truly fantastical artichokes-inspired creature, as it began to wander, its powerful kickstarting a new era, securing the ultimate goal.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A young woman with short, sharp purple hair and cybernetic arm glowing with turquoise light, wearing a distressed leather jacket and high-tech glasses. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The young woman (protagonist) stands before a grand, glowing, synthetic unicorn, its form intricate and mechanical, in a vast, sterile laboratory. Wide shot, slightly from above. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist confronts a shadowy figure on a rain-slicked neon-lit urban freeway, their faces obscured by the harsh reflections. Medium shot, eye-level. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist is shown making a precise, technical adjustment to the unicorn's internal wiring, sparks flying, her cybernetic arm glowing intensely. Close-up on hands and arm, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The fully restored, magnificent synthetic unicorn gallops through a fantastical, bioluminescent forest as the protagonist watches from a nearby walkway, a sense of wonder and triumph on her face. Dynamic wide shot, slightly from below.

definitions.definition: The master of the servants of a household; the master of a feudal manor

One possessing similar mastery over others; any feudal superior generally; any nobleman or aristocrat; any chief, prince, or sovereign ruler; in Scotland, a male member of the lowest rank of nobility (the equivalent rank in England is baron)
One possessing similar mastery in figurative senses (esp. as lord of ~)
The heavenly body considered to possess a dominant influence over an event, time, etc.
A hunchback.
(via Cockney rhyming slang) Sixpence.
Domineer or act like a lord.
To invest with the dignity, power, and privileges of a lord; to grant the title of lord.
Inside of something
definitions.example: We saw the internal compartments.

definitions.definition: Within the body
definitions.example: Her bleeding was internal.

definitions.definition: Concerned with the domestic affairs of a nation, state or other political community.
definitions.example: The nation suffered from internal conflicts.

definitions.definition: Concerned with the non-public affairs of a company or other organisation
definitions.example: An internal investigation was conducted.

definitions.definition: A challenge to the admission of a juror, without the challenger needing to show good cause.
Precluding debate or expostulation; not admitting of question or appeal
Positive in opinion or judgment; absolutely certain, overconfident, unwilling to hear any debate or argument (especially in a pejorative sense); dogmatic.
Firmly determined, resolute; obstinate, stubborn.
Accepting no refusal or disagreement; imperious, dictatorial.
In the Church of England, the priest of a parish, receiving a salary or stipend but not tithes.
In the Roman Catholic and some other churches, a cleric acting as local representative of a higher ranking member of the clergy.
A person acting on behalf of, or representing, another person.
Tending to talk excessively about oneself.
Believing oneself to be better and more important than others.
Egoistical.
Someone who is skilled in their trade; an artisan.
An inventor.
A member of the military who specializes in manufacturing and repairing weapon systems.
A trickster.
A savant.
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
Like a fairy; fanciful, whimsical, delicate.
To press; to push; to drive; to impel; to force onward.
To press the mind or will of; to ply with motives, arguments, persuasion, or importunity.
To provoke; to exasperate.
To press hard upon; to follow closely.
To present in an urgent manner; to insist upon.
definitions.example: to urge an argument; to urge the necessity of a case

definitions.definition: To treat with forcible means; to take severe or violent measures with.
definitions.example: to urge an ore with intense heat

definitions.definition: To press onward or forward.
To be pressing in argument; to insist; to persist.
A mythical beast resembling a horse or deer with a single, straight, spiraled horn projecting from its forehead.
In various Bible translations, used to render the Latin unicornis or rhinoceros (representing Hebrew רְאֵם): a reem or wild ox.
Any large beetle having a horn-like prominence on the head or prothorax, especially the Hercules beetle, Dynastes tityus.
A caterpillar, Schizura unicornis, with a large thorn-like spine on the back near its head.
The kamichi, or unicorn bird.
A howitzer.
Someone or something that is rare and hard to find.
Being many (especially pastel) colours; multicoloured.
definitions.example: unicorn smoothies

definitions.definition: A 15th-century Scottish gold coin worth 18 shillings, bearing the image of a unicorn.
(sexual slang) To participate in a sexual threesome as a bisexual addition to an established heterosexual couple.
To exceed a valuation of one billion U.S. dollars, while solely backed by venture capitalists.
A student at a university who has not yet received a degree.
Of, relating to, or being an undergraduate.
definitions.example: After completing my undergraduate studies, I embarked on a career in publishing.

definitions.definition: A large and violent whirlpool.
Any violent or turbulent situation.
Any small rodent of the genus Mus.
A member of the many small rodent and marsupial species resembling such a rodent.
A quiet or shy person.
(plural mice or, rarely, mouses) An input device that is moved over a pad or other flat surface to produce a corresponding movement of a pointer on a graphical display.
Hematoma.
A turn or lashing of spun yarn or small stuff, or a metallic clasp or fastening, uniting the point and shank of a hook to prevent its unhooking or straightening out.
A familiar term of endearment.
A match used in firing guns or blasting.
A small model of (a fragment of) Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory with desirable properties (depending on the context).
A small cushion for a woman's hair.
To move cautiously or furtively, in the manner of a mouse (the rodent) (frequently used in the phrasal verb to mouse around).
To hunt or catch mice (the rodents), usually of cats.
To close the mouth of a hook by a careful binding of marline or wire.
definitions.example: Captain Higgins moused the hook with a bit of marline to prevent the block beckets from falling out under slack.

definitions.definition: To navigate by means of a computer mouse.
To tear, as a cat devours a mouse.
A small fighting force specially trained for making quick destructive raids against enemy-held areas.
A commando trooper
An organized force of Boer troops in South Africa; a raid by such troops
A result that one is attempting to achieve.
definitions.example: My lifelong goal is to get into a Hollywood movie.

definitions.definition: In many sports, an area into which the players attempt to put an object.
The act of placing the object into the goal.
A point scored in a game as a result of placing the object into the goal.
A noun or noun phrase that receives the action of a verb. The subject of a passive verb or the direct object of an active verb. Also called a patient, target, or undergoer.
To score a goal.
A mammal of the order Primates, including simians and prosimians.

definitions.definition: A simian anthropoid; an ape, human or monkey.

definitions.definition: Any preparation to be applied to the hair, skin, nails, etc.

definitions.definition: Anything that is produced; a result.

definitions.definition: Illegal drugs, especially cocaine, when viewed as a commodity.

definitions.definition: To produce.
An individual who is on strike.
Someone or something that hits someone or something else.
One of the players on a team in football (soccer) in the row nearest to the opposing team's goal, who are therefore principally responsible for scoring goals.

(1800s) The batter.

An entry in such a book.

A certificate issued by the collector of customs of a port or district to the owner of a vessel, containing the description of a vessel, its name, ownership, and other material facts. It is kept on board the vessel, to be used as evidence of nationality or as a muniment of title.

To enter in a register.
To enroll, especially to vote.
To record, especially in writing.
To buy the full version of trial software by providing one's details and payment.

To make or adjust so as to be properly or precisely aligned.

At a future time.

Restless and intolerant of delays.

Not to be borne; unendurable.
Prompted by, or exhibiting, impatience.

A pale greenish-blue colour, like that of the gemstone.
Made of turquoise (the gemstone).
Having a pale greenish-blue colour.
A single, distinctive rock formation; an area having a preponderance of a particular rock or group of rocks.
An area of land or the particular features of it.

definitions.definition: A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.
A neighborhood or near place; the place or county in which anything is alleged to have happened; also, the place where an action is laid, or the district from which a jury comes.
A bout; a hit; a turn. See venew.
Sport venue: a stadium or similar building in which a sporting competition is held.
One of the hairs which grow along the edge of eyelids
To dig or excavate a trench; to trench.

definitions.definition: To establish a substantial position in business, politics, etc.

To give (an award).
definitions.example: Four or five of these medals are awarded every year.

definitions.example: He was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature.

definitions.definition: An inadequate person.
Not worthy; lacking value or merit; worthless.

definitions.example: Killing doctors is barbaric.

definitions.definition: An ongoing relationship between (usually two) rivals who compete for superiority.
definitions.example: James and Polly have a bitter rivalry due to the latter's inclusion in the Tea Time Teaser.

definitions.definition: The characteristic of being a rivalrous good, such that it can be consumed or used by only one person at a time.
definitions.example: Common goods are those which are rivalrous in consumption but non-excludable

definitions.definition: Any competition between two or more things or factors.

definitions.definition: To cause to become numb (physically or emotionally).

definitions.example: He turned to alcohol to numb his pain.

The dockland district of a town.

definitions.definition: Excessively careful; cautious.
Malignant; mischievous; spiteful.

Structural resemblances between minerals; resemblances that suggest that they are of a common origin or type.

A toll-free highway.

A connected group.

A battle, a fight (often one in which weapons are used).

To fight; to struggle against.

definitions.definition: To fight (with); to struggle for victory (against).
Ice hockey, a game on ice in which two teams of six players skate and try to score by shooting a puck into the opposing team's net, using their sticks.
Field hockey, a team sport played on a pitch on solid ground where players have to hit a ball into a net using a hockey stick.

A ball delivered with a twist, as in cricket or billiards.
A tornado.

The inner part of the thigh, the proper place to rest upon when on horseback.

The party game Twister, usually capitalized, or a variant.
In a way that is experienced in the imagination through the actions of another person. "she was living vicariously through her children"; by proxy.
Wages, money earned, income.
Business profits.
Gains on investments; returns.
An incident, action, or time period standing out by itself, but more or less connected with a complete series of events.
definitions.example: It was a most embarrassing episode in my life.

definitions.definition: An instalment of a drama told in parts, as in a TV series.
definitions.example: I can't wait till next week’s episode.

definitions.definition: One who keeps something.
definitions.example: Finders keepers; losers weepers].

definitions.definition: A person or thing worth keeping.
A person charged with guarding or caring for, storing, or maintaining something; a custodian, a guard; sometimes a gamekeeper.
The player charged with guarding a goal or wicket. Short form of goalkeeper, wicketkeeper.
A part of a mechanism that catches or retains another part, for example the part of a door lock that fits in the frame and receives the bolt.
An offensive play in which the quarterback runs toward the goal with the ball after it is snapped.
One who remains or keeps in a place or position.
A fruit or vegetable that keeps for some time without spoiling.

A person born in the postwar years (generally considered in the United States and other Allied countries as between 1946 and the early 1960s), when there was a marked increase in birthrates throughout the Western world following the return of servicemen at the end of World War II.
An adult male kangaroo.
(by extension) A person who does not know how to utilize new technologies well.
definitions.example: I'm such a boomer; I have no idea how to use this phone.

definitions.definition: Something that makes a booming sound.
(mainly 1920–1930) A transient worker who would move from boom town to boom town in search of temporary work.
A device used to bind or tighten chain.
A nuclear ballistic missile submarine, SSBN.
A bittern (subfamily Botaurinae).
A red squirrel (Tamiasciurus hudsonicus).
A mountain beaver (Aplodontia rufa).
A type of flattish bun, usually cut in two horizontally, toasted and spread with butter, etc, before being eaten.
A type of individual bread such as corn, bran, banana or zucchini bread often sliced and spread with butter, etc before being eaten.
(especially US) (informally) A cupcake without frosting, but sometimes glazed.
A mechanism used in the Java Network Launching Protocol analogous to the cookie mechanism and which permits a program running in a browser to perform operations on a client machine.
Term of endearment.
definitions.example: I love you, muffin!

definitions.definition: (sexual slang) A vulva.
A less talented player; one who muffs, or drops the ball.
A charming, attractive young man.
A small plate, smaller than a twiffler.
Of, or relating to the nerves, neurons or the nervous system.
Modelled on the arrangement of neurons in the brain.
(money) A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
A token used in a special establishment like a casino.
That which serves for payment or recompense.
Money in general, not limited to coins.
definitions.example: She spent some serious coin on that car!

definitions.definition: One of the suits of minor arcana in tarot, or a card of that suit.
A corner or external angle.
A small circular slice of food.
A cryptocurrency.
definitions.example: What's the best coin to buy right now?

definitions.definition: To make of a definite fineness, and convert into coins, as a mass of metal.
definitions.example: to coin a medal

definitions.definition: (by extension) To make or fabricate.
definitions.example: Over the last century the advance in science has led to many new words being coined.

definitions.definition: To acquire rapidly, as money; to make.

To behave as a jaywalker; to violate pedestrian traffic regulations by crossing a street away from a designated crossing or to walk in the part of the street intended for vehicles rather than on the sidewalk.
A powered watercraft with a seat and handlebars in a style similar to a motorbike.
To ride on a jetski.
Intended or planned; done deliberately or voluntarily.
Done with intent.
Unfaithfulness in a marriage or an intimate relationship: practice or instance of having a sexual or romantic affair with someone other than one's spouse, without the consent of the spouse.
Unfaithfulness in some other moral obligation.
Lack of religious belief.
Causing trouble or anxiety
definitions.example: The computer has been very troublesome for me. It never works when I need to use it.

definitions.definition: To engage in trade.
definitions.example: This company trades (in) precious metal.

definitions.definition: To be traded at a certain price or under certain conditions.
definitions.example: Apple is trading at $200.

definitions.definition: To give (something) in exchange for.
definitions.example: Will you trade your precious watch for my earring?

definitions.definition: To give someone a plant and receive a different one in return.
To do business; offer for sale as for one's livelihood.
To have dealings; to be concerned or associated (with).
The carrying on of trade.
Carrying on trade or commerce; engaged in trade.
definitions.example: a trading company

definitions.definition: Frequented by traders.
Venal; corrupt; jobbing.
definitions.example: a trading politician

definitions.definition: To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.
To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
definitions.example: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

definitions.definition: A small four-stringed guitar.
The act of coming up.
Comeuppance; deserts
Happening or appearing in the relatively near future.
definitions.example: The Federal budget lays out government spending for the upcoming budget year.

definitions.definition: Something opposite or contrary to something else.
An opponent.
An antonym.
definitions.example: "Up" is the opposite of "down".

definitions.definition: An additive inverse.
Located directly across from something else, or from each other.
definitions.example: She saw him walking on the opposite side of the road.

definitions.definition: Of leaves and flowers, positioned directly across from each other on a stem.
Facing in the other direction.
definitions.example: They were moving in opposite directions.

definitions.definition: Of either of two complementary or mutually exclusive things.
definitions.example: He is attracted to the opposite sex.

definitions.definition: Extremely different; inconsistent; contrary; repugnant; antagonistic.
In an opposite position.
definitions.example: I was on my seat and she stood opposite.

definitions.definition: Facing, or across from.
definitions.example: He lives opposite the pub.

definitions.definition: In a complementary role to.
definitions.example: He played opposite Marilyn Monroe.

definitions.definition: On another channel at the same time.
definitions.example: The game show Just Men! aired opposite The Young and the Restless on CBS.

definitions.definition: A tropical Asian fruit tree, Mangifera indica.
The fruit of the mango tree.
A pickled vegetable or fruit with a spicy stuffing; a vegetable or fruit which has been mangoed.
(chiefly southern Midwest US) A green bell pepper suitable for pickling.
A type of muskmelon, Cucumis melo.
Any of various hummingbirds of the genus Anthracothorax.
A yellow-orange color, like that of mango flesh.
To stuff and pickle (a fruit).
A single droplet of rainwater that has just fallen or is falling from the sky.
To catch and enclose or compress tightly between two surfaces, or points which are brought together or closed; to pinch; to close in upon.
To remove by pinching, biting, or cutting with two meeting edges of anything; to clip.
To blast, as by frost; to check the growth or vigor of; to destroy.
To annoy, as by nipping.
To taunt.
To squeeze or pinch.
To steal; especially to cut a purse.
To make a quick, short journey or errand, usually a round trip.
definitions.example: Why don’t you nip down to the grocer’s for some milk?

definitions.definition: A quick stab or blow; a poking or thrusting motion.
A short straight punch.
A medical injection.
definitions.example: Our dog was exposed to rabies, so the whole family went to a clinic to get our jabs.

definitions.definition: A vaccination, whether or not delivered via conventional injection.
A mild verbal insult.
To poke or thrust abruptly, or to make such a motion.
To deliver a quick punch.
To give someone an injection
A desire, hope, or longing for something or for something to happen.
An expression of such a desire, often connected with ideas of magic and supernatural power.
The thing desired or longed for.
definitions.example: My dearest wish is to see them happily married.

definitions.definition: A water meadow.
To desire; to want.
definitions.example: I'll come tomorrow, if you wish it.

definitions.definition: To hope (+ object clause with may or in present subjunctive).
(followed by for) To hope (for a particular outcome).
(ditransitive) To bestow (a thought or gesture) towards (someone or something).
definitions.example: We wish you a Merry Christmas.

definitions.definition: (followed by to and an infinitive) To request or desire to do an activity.
To recommend; to seek confidence or favour on behalf of.
A clearly defined path for pedestrians.
The act or instance of wandering.
definitions.example: to go for a wander in the park

definitions.definition: The situation where a value or signal etc. deviates from the correct or normal value.
definitions.example: baseline wander in ECG signals

definitions.definition: To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.
definitions.example: to wander over the fields

definitions.definition: To stray; stray from one's course; err.
definitions.example: A writer wanders from his subject.

definitions.definition: To commit adultery.
To go somewhere indirectly or at varying speeds; to move in a curved path.
Of the mind, to lose focus or clarity of argument or attention.
A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.
A clear path through rows of seating.
A clear corridor in a supermarket with shelves on both sides containing goods for sale.
Any path through an otherwise obstructed space.
Seat in public transport, such as a plane, train or bus, that's beside the aisle.
definitions.example: Do you want to seat window or aisle?

definitions.definition: An idiomatic divide between the Republican Party and the Democratic Party, who are said to be on two sides of the aisle.
A (small) island, compare with islet.
An artist who paints pictures.
A laborer or workman who paints surfaces using a paintbrush or other means.
A chain or rope used to attach the shank of an anchor to the side of a ship when not in use.
A rope connected to the bow of a boat, used to attach it to e.g. a jetty or another boat.
A mountain lion.
The extent to which time is well used for the intended task.
definitions.example: The efficiency of the planning department is deplorable.

definitions.definition: The quality of producing an effect or effects.
The extent to which a resource, such as electricity, is used for the intended purpose; the ratio of useful work to energy expended.
definitions.example: The efficiency of this loudspeaker is 40%.

definitions.definition: A one-room apartment.
definitions.example: I have an efficiency available June through July.

definitions.definition: To stir the emotions of.
definitions.example: The fireworks which opened the festivities excited anyone present.

definitions.definition: To arouse or bring out (e.g. feelings); to stimulate.
definitions.example: Favoritism tends to excite jealousy in the ones not being favored.

definitions.definition: To cause an electron to move to a higher than normal state; to promote an electron to an outer level.
definitions.example: By applying electric potential to the neon atoms, the electrons become excited, then emit a photon when returning to normal.

definitions.definition: To energize (an electromagnet); to produce a magnetic field in.
definitions.example: to excite a dynamo

definitions.definition: The process of something becoming excited; excitation.
Creating or producing excitement
A plant related to the thistle with enlarged flower heads eaten as a vegetable while immature, Cynara cardunculus var. scolymus.
A dull green colour, like that of an artichoke.
The movement of the electron beam back to its initial position between scan lines in a cathode-ray tube display.
A special kind of electrical transformer, originally designed to generate high-voltage sawtooth signals at a relatively high frequency, and now extensively used in switched-mode power supplies.
One who obeys.
Willing to comply with the commands, orders, or instructions of those in authority.
definitions.example: Jessica was so intensely obedient of her parents that her brother sometimes thought she was a robot.

definitions.definition: The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later at will.
definitions.example: Memory is a facility common to all animals.

definitions.definition: A record of a thing or an event stored and available for later use by the organism.
definitions.example: I have no memory of that event.

definitions.definition: The part of a computer that stores variable executable code or data (RAM) or unalterable executable code or default data (ROM).
definitions.example: This data passes from the CPU to the memory.

definitions.definition: The time within which past events can be or are remembered.
definitions.example: in recent memory; in living memory

definitions.definition: (of a material) which returns to its original shape when heated
definitions.example: Memory metal; memory plastic.

definitions.definition: A memorial.
(collective) A term of venery for a social group of elephants, normally called a herd.
The act of sending someone away.
Deprivation of office; the fact or process of being fired from employment or stripped of rank.
A written or spoken statement of such an act.
Release from confinement; liberation.
Removal from consideration; putting something out of one's mind, mentally disregarding something or someone.
The rejection of a legal proceeding, or a claim or charge made therein.
The event of a batsman getting out; a wicket.
The bending of a wave around an obstacle.
The breaking up of an electromagnetic wave as it passes a geometric structure (e.g. a slit), followed by reconstruction of the wave by interference.
Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
definitions.example: The room was too dark for reading.

definitions.definition: (of colour) Dull or deeper in hue; not bright or light.
definitions.example: my sister's hair is darker than mine;  her skin grew dark with a suntan

definitions.definition: Hidden, secret, obscure.
Without moral or spiritual light; sinister, malign.
definitions.example: a dark villain;  a dark deed

definitions.definition: Conducive to hopelessness; depressing or bleak.
definitions.example: the Great Depression was a dark time;  the film was a dark psychological thriller

definitions.definition: Lacking progress in science or the arts; said of a time period.
With emphasis placed on the unpleasant aspects of life; said of a work of fiction, a work of nonfiction presented in narrative form or a portion of either.
definitions.example: The ending of this book is rather dark.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to numbers
The same in number; hence, identically the same; identical.
A room, building or institution equipped for scientific research, experimentation or analysis.
A place where chemicals, drugs or microbes are prepared or manufactured.
A peddler, huckster, who travels about to sell easily transportable goods.
Any dragonfly of the family Aeshnidae; a darner.
Someone who breeds and trains hawks and other falcons; a falconer.
Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: The tree of the species Citrus paradisi, a hybrid of pomelo (Citrus maxima) and sweet orange.
The large spherical tart fruit produced by this tree.
(sometimes derogatory) A homeless person; a vagabond.
A disreputable, promiscuous woman; a slut.
definitions.example: "Claudia is such a tramp; making out with all those men when she has a boyfriend."

definitions.definition: Any ship which does not have a fixed schedule or published ports of call.
A long walk, possibly of more than one day, in a scenic or wilderness area.
(in apposition) Of objects, stray and intrusive and unwanted
A metal plate worn by diggers under the hollow of the foot to save the shoe.
To walk with heavy footsteps.
To walk for a long time (usually through difficult terrain).
definitions.example: We tramped through the woods for hours before we found the main path again.

definitions.definition: To hitchhike.
To tread upon forcibly and repeatedly; to trample.
To travel or wander through.
definitions.example: to tramp the country

definitions.definition: To cleanse, as clothes, by treading upon them in water.
A gymnastic and recreational device consisting of a piece of taut, strong fabric stretched over a steel frame using many coiled springs as anchors.
Any of a variety of looping or jumping instructions in specific programming languages
A property which is not essential; a nonessential; anything happening accidentally.
Those fortuitous effects produced by luminous rays falling on certain objects so that some parts stand forth in abnormal brightness and other parts are cast into a deep shadow.
A sharp, flat, or natural, occurring not at the commencement of a piece of music as the signature, but before a particular note.
Part of a text that has a mainly structural purpose, such as spelling, punctuation or capitalization.
Not essential; incidental, secondary.
Nonessential to something's inherent nature (especially in Aristotelian thought).
Adjusted by one or two semitones, in temporary departure from the key signature.
Occurring sometimes, by chance; occasional.
Happening by chance, or unexpectedly; taking place not according to the usual course of things; by accident, unintentional.
Being a double point with two distinct tangent planes in 4-dimensional projective space.
To start a motorcycle.
definitions.example: He kick started the motorcycle and took off.

definitions.definition: To start or begin strongly or quickly.
definitions.example: Kick start your day with a hearty breakfast.

definitions.definition: To make (something) active, functional, or productive again; to reinvigorate (an activity, system, or process).
definitions.example: To kick start the economy with a large construction project.

definitions.definition: The spillage resultant from overflow; excess.
Outlet for escape of excess material.
The situation where a value exceeds the available numeric range.
To flow over the brim of (a container).
definitions.example: The river overflowed the levee.

definitions.definition: To cover with a liquid, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: The flash flood overflowed most of the parkland and some homes.

definitions.definition: To cause an overflow.
To flow over the edge of a container.
definitions.example: The waters overflowed into the Ninth Ward.

definitions.definition: To exceed limits or capacity.
definitions.example: The hospital ER was overflowing with flu cases.

definitions.definition: To be superabundant; to abound.
To dull or reduce an emotion or a physical state.
Blunt; not sharp, pointed, or acute in form.
Intellectually dull or dim-witted.
Of sound, etc.: deadened, muffled, muted.
Indirect or circuitous.
